title: Pouteria shweliensis
intro: Pouteria shweliensis, also known as the Shweli tree, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The health benefits of Pouteria shweliensis are attributed to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in managing various health conditions such as arthritis, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pouteria shweliensis is commonly used in traditional medicine and is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Pouteria shweliensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, which is a group of evergreen trees and shrubs native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The Sapotaceae family is part of the larger order Ericales, a diverse group of flowering plants that also includes heath families and several families of woody and herbaceous plants. Pouteria shweliensis is further classified as a member of the genus Pouteria, a group of flowering plants that are often known as sapodilla or sapote. The genus Pouteria is thought to have originated in the Americas, but species within the genus are now found in various parts of the world, including Africa and Asia. The classification of Pouteria shweliensis highlights its unique position within the diverse Sapotaceae family and its connection to other flowering plants around the world.
